title: opt_clean removes $_BUF_ cells with keep attribute
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

Verilog file (test.v):
```verilog
module test (
    input i,
    output o
);
    wire w1;
    wire w2;

    assign w1 = ~i;
    assign w2 = w1;
    assign o = ~w2;
endmodule
```

Yosys script:
```
read_verilog test.v
insbuf
# select just the $_BUF_ from w1 to w2 as @buf
select -set buf w:w1 %coe1 w:w1 %d
# set the keep attribute for the $_BUF_ from w1 to w2
setattr -set keep 1 @buf
opt_clean
stat
```

## Expected behavior

I expect the `$_BUF_` cell with the keep attribute to remain after `opt_clean`, so I would expect `stat` to show 1 `$_BUF_` cell.

## Actual behavior

All the `$_BUF_` cells are removed by `opt_clean`, and `stat` shows no `$_BUF_` cells.

## Additional notes

I ran into this because I wanted to use `submod` to extract a submodule from a circuit where one of the outputs of the submodule was going to be directly connected to one of the inputs (for example, making a submodule using `w1` and `w2` in my example Verilog above). Selecting just the wire doesn't get the desired effect with `submod`, so I added a buffer with `insbuf`. Unfortunately `submod` runs `opt_clean` as its first step so the buffer is removed even when it is marked as keep.

I was able to fix this myself by changing https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/blob/master/passes/opt/opt_clean.cc#L483 to
```c++
if (cell->type.in("$pos", "$_BUF_") && !cell->has_keep_attr()) {
```
I would have sent a PR for this, but I'm not sure if ignoring keep attributes on `$_BUF_` cells is intended behavior or not. If it is intended, would you it be okay to add a command line argument to `submod` that skips `opt_clean`?
